summary.intent: Fix all UI strings and validation logic that still referenced the old 8-character minimum password requirement, updating them to reflect the current 12-character minimum enforced by the backend.
summary.outcome: Updated 16 occurrences across 9 files (EN/FR locale files, JS view files, Swift bridge, macOS app, and docs) to reference 12 characters. All changes were linted, committed, and the grep confirmed no remaining '8 characters' references.

repo: Password minimum length is enforced as 12 in auth_routes.rs (backend) but was inconsistently reflected across frontend locales, JS views, Swift bridge, macOS app, and documentation.
Locale strings are split by language (en/fr) and by section (settings.js, onboarding.js) under crates/web/src/assets/js/locales/.
Hardcoded password length strings exist in both locale files AND directly in onboarding-view.js and page-settings.js — two separate layers to update.
Swift bridge validation (ffi_auth.rs) duplicates the password length check independently from the Rust backend (auth_routes.rs).
Biome is used for JS linting/formatting in this repo.

code.finding: passwordPlaceholder string had hardcoded '8 characters' — the original review comment pointed to this line (ea9fc8d).
Swift bridge independently validates password length with a hardcoded value, separate from backend auth_routes.rs — both needed updating.
macOS app also had a hardcoded password length reference requiring update.

workflow: Initial edit attempts failed because files weren't read first — the Edit tool requires a prior Read in the conversation.
Grep verification after edits ('8 characters' returning no results) is an effective final check for this class of find-and-replace task.
Running biome lint/format after bulk JS edits before committing is the established practice here.

friction: First round of Edit tool calls failed because the files had not been read first, requiring a second pass of reads before edits could proceed — added significant redundant steps.
Swift bridge generates missing types that cause pre-existing diagnostics, making it hard to distinguish new issues from pre-existing noise.
open_items: The password minimum length (12) is hardcoded in multiple places (backend, Swift bridge, macOS app, locale strings, JS views, docs) with no single source of truth — a shared constant would prevent this class of drift.
French locale files were updated but no native speaker review was mentioned — translation accuracy for the new '12 characters' phrasing should be verified.
